title: Privacy Policy
summary: This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, when we share it, and the choices available to you when you use DotKE services.
highlights: Explains what information we collect and why we need it.
Describes how we protect, store, and share personal information.
Outlines the privacy choices available when using DotKE services.

sections.title: Scope
body: This Privacy Policy explains how DotKE collects, uses, stores, and shares personal information when people use the app, website, support channels, and related domain-management services.
By using our services, you acknowledge that your information may be processed as described in this policy.
bullets: Applies to customer, prospect, and support interactions.
Covers web, mobile, and operational support workflows.

sections.title: Information We Collect
body: We may collect account details, profile information, contact information, payment references, device identifiers, support messages, and event logs needed to secure and operate the service.
We also collect technical diagnostics that help us detect abuse, investigate failures, and improve product reliability.
bullets: Identity and contact details such as name, email, and phone number.
Domain and transaction records such as search history, orders, and status changes.
Security and device telemetry such as IP address, device type, and authentication events.

sections.title: How We Use Information
body: We use personal information to provide the service, complete transactions, secure accounts, communicate with users, meet legal obligations, and improve system performance.
Where the law requires it, we rely on consent or another valid legal basis for processing.
bullets: To provision and manage products and services.
To prevent fraud, account takeover, abuse, and other harmful activity.
To provide support, notices, and service updates.

sections.title: Sharing and Service Providers
body: We may share information with registrars, payment processors, infrastructure providers, analytics providers, regulators, or professional advisers when required to operate the service or comply with law.
When providers act on our behalf, we expect them to process information only for the services they support and to apply appropriate security controls.
bullets: We do not share personal data casually or without purpose.
We expect vendors to use appropriate confidentiality and security safeguards.

sections.title: Security
body: We use administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction.
No system is perfectly secure, so our controls include monitoring, access management, audit logging, and incident response practices.
bullets: Encryption in transit where appropriate.
Role-based access and privileged action controls.
Operational monitoring and forensic logging for high-risk events.

sections.title: Data Retention
body: We retain information for as long as necessary to provide the service, meet legal or regulatory obligations, resolve disputes, enforce agreements, and protect the integrity of our systems.
Retention periods should reflect the type of record, the business purpose, and the applicable legal requirements.
bullets: Operational logs may be retained longer for security review and audit.
Records no longer needed should be deleted or anonymized where practical.

sections.title: Your Rights and Choices
body: Depending on applicable law, users may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to certain processing of their personal information.
Before acting on a request, we may ask for reasonable verification to protect your information and prevent unauthorized access.
bullets: Users may be able to request access, correction, deletion, or restriction depending on applicable law.
We may ask for additional verification before acting on a request.

sections.title: Cross-Border Processing
body: Some service providers or infrastructure may operate outside the user's home jurisdiction. When that happens, we expect appropriate transfer safeguards and security controls to be in place.
When information is processed across borders, we use reasonable safeguards designed to protect personal information.
bullets: Vendor review should include storage location and access boundaries.
High-risk processing requires stronger contractual and technical review.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
body: We may update this policy to reflect service changes, legal developments, or improved explanations. Material changes should include a clear notice where appropriate.
When changes are important, we may provide additional notice through the app, website, or support channels.
bullets: Review changes through a controlled release process.
Publish policy updates through a controlled release process.
